IAW FAR 12 The Government intends to make a source selection as part of a HUBZone competitive set-aside utilizing the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) process. The LPTA process does not permit tradeoffs among price and non-price factors. Offerors shall assume that the Government has no prior knowledge of their facilities and experience. The Government will base its evaluation on the information presented in the Offeror's proposal.
Factor 1. Management/Technical Approach.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's management approach to determine if the proposed approach demonstrates an understanding of the need for a highly qualified, stable contractor workforce and will result in a highly qualified, stable contractor workforce capable of performing awarded TO requirements.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's Technical Approach to determine if the offeror has demonstrated the ability to successfully perform and a clear understanding of the PWS Task Areas in the OPI field.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's proposed Staffing and Management Approach to determine whether the offeror has proposed a comprehensive approach to plan, execute, and manage a task order and to provide qualified key personnel.

Contract Number: 75H71324R00058
Agency: National Institutes of Health (NIH), National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)
Title: Biomedical Laboratory Equipment Preventive Maintenance and Repair Services
NAICS: 811210 – Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance
Size Standard: $34.0M

Contract Type: Firm-Fixed-Price (FFP), Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ)

AMENDMENT OF SOLICITATION/MODIFICATION OF CONTRACT
U.S. Department of Agriculture – Agricultural Research Service
Solicitation No.: AG-6395-S-25-0021
Amendment No.: 0003

Purpose of Amendment: This amendment is issued to clarify the set-aside determination and to incorporate additional questions and answers from the pre-proposal conference held on March 12, 2025.

1. SET-ASIDE CLARIFICATION:

The Contracting Officer confirms that this acquisition for Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Software Development and Support Services is set aside for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns under the WOSB Program, as authorized by FAR 19.1505. This is NOT an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside. The solicitation is restricted to WOSB-certified firms only.

NAICS: 541511 – Custom Computer Programming Services
Size Standard: $34.0 million

Offerors must hold a current, valid WOSB certification issued by the SBA at the time of offer and at the time of award. The firm must be unconditionally owned and controlled by one or more women who are U.S. citizens, and must be small under the assigned NAICS code. The clause at FAR 52.219-29, Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole-Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program, is applicable and is hereby incorporated by reference.

2. QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS:

Q7: Can a joint venture between a WOSB and a large business submit a proposal?
A7: No. Only firms that are themselves certified WOSB concerns under the SBA's WOSB Program are eligible. Joint ventures must independently qualify as WOSB concerns and meet the requirements of 13 CFR 127.506.

Q8: Is past performance under prior EDWOSB contracts acceptable as relevant experience?
A8: Yes, past performance references from EDWOSB or WOSB contracts are acceptable as relevant experience for evaluation under Factor 2.

All other terms and conditions remain unchanged.

2.0 Task Order 01 Scope

The Post Award Kickoff Conference will serve as the official launch of the NITE-STAR Capability Development MA IDIQ. The purpose is to achieve a clear and mutual understanding of contract requirements, establish communication channels, and set expectations for successful contract execution. Absolutely no changes to the contract will be made, nor contract terms renegotiated. The meeting will also serve as a forum for questions and clarifications.

2.1 Post Award Kickoff Conference

2.1.1 Each Contractor shall participate in a Post Award Kickoff Conference, to be held in Colorado Springs, CO no later than 30 days after contract award. The Post Award Kickoff Conference will be scheduled for approximately 90 minutes, conducted face-to-face, and will not include other contractors from the IDIQ contractor pool.

2.1.2 Each Contractor will be awarded a firm-fixed price TO valued at $10,000 to complete TO 01.

BASIS FOR AWARD: This award selection will be conducted using Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) procedures. To be eligible for an award, a proposal must meet all technical requirements, comply with all required terms and conditions, and include all required information. All non-price evaluation factors must be rated as "ACCEPTABLE" in order to be eligible for award. An "UNACCEPTABLE" rating in any factor will result in the proposal being rated "UNACCEPTABLE" unless corrected through discussions. A proposal rating of "UNACCEPTABLE" makes a proposal ineligible for award. Since the LPTA methodology prohibits trade-offs, all evaluation factors and sub-factors are equally important. Proposals are evaluated for acceptability but not ranked based on non-price factors.

A price evaluation will be performed for all offerors, and proposals will be ranked from lowest to highest price based on the Total Proposed Price (TPP) provided by the offeror on the price schedule sheet. Next, each factor will be evaluated and, subsequently, the overall proposal will be prepared, beginning with the lowest-priced proposal. The award will be made to the lowest-priced proposal that meets the acceptability standards for the non-price factors.

SECTION M - EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD

In order to permit efficient competition and evaluation, the Navy may utilize the following methodology. Quotes will initially be assessed for price and ranked in order (lowest priced to highest priced). The Navy will then evaluate the technical factors of the lowest priced quote. In the event that this quote is deemed Unacceptable, the next lowest priced quote will be evaluated as many times as necessary, until the Government identifies a technically acceptable quote. The Navy will make award to the lowest priced technically acceptable quote, if possible. Accordingly, under this methodology, the technical factors of some quotes may not be evaluated by the Navy. The Government reserves the right to evaluate additional higher priced quoters to support a fair and reasonableness determination or for other considerations. If exchanges are deemed necessary by the Contracting Officer, a maximum of five (5) lowest priced quotes will be evaluated (both non-price and price) for the purpose of establishing a competitive range.

M-215-H004 BASIS OF AWARD (NAVSEA) (DEC 2018)

All units of all items will be awarded to one offeror. The resulting contract will be awarded to the low priced offeror who is deemed responsible in accordance with the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 9, as supplemented, whose proposal conforms to the solicitation's requirements and based on the evaluation factors is considered technically acceptable. Technical acceptability is determined by the offeror demonstrating the material/service being provided meets the solicitation requirements. The low offer will be analyzed to determine if the offeror's proposed price is fair and reasonable utilizing price analysis techniques in accordance with the guidelines in FAR Subpart 13.106-3.

Evaluation Process and Basis of Award
The purchase order resulting from this solicitation will be issued as a FIRM-FIXED PRICE purchase order. The purchase order will be made to the LOWEST-PRICE technically acceptable quote.
The USCG intends to award the purchase order based on the initial quote without engaging in exchanges. However, the USCG reserves the right to conduct exchanges with one or more of the vendors if determined by the Contracting Officer to be necessary and in the best interests of the USCG. The USCG may reject any or all quotes if such action is in the public interest and waive informalities and minor irregularities in quotes received. Vendors are strongly encouraged to submit their best quote in response to this solicitation.
The Government will first review the quotes for completeness/compliance (everything required to be submitted has been submitted). If a quote is found to be incomplete/non-compliant, it may be removed from the evaluation process, and it will not be further evaluated. Then the Government will list the remaining quotes by price, lowest to highest, and will begin the technical quote evaluation with the lowest priced Offeror. Upon finding the Offeror who meets the technical acceptability, the Offeror's price will be evaluated for reasonableness. If the first quote evaluated is technically acceptable with a price that is reasonable, evaluation will stop. If the first quote evaluated is either not technically acceptable or has a price that is not reasonable evaluation will continue. Once the lowest reasonably priced, technically acceptable quote is found evaluations will stop.

The software development for CDM should be anchored in SAFe Agile framework and DevSecOps methodologies. The program will decompose requirements into epics, capabilities, and user stories, ensuring that development teams can deliver working software at a rapid cadence aligning with SAFe Agile methodology. • Identification and definition of case development product requirements (e.g. lightweight capability definitions, user-stories) based on both technical documentation and User-Feedback obtained through Human Centered Design and Agile processes.
• Assistance in the prioritization and planning of software work items within DSCA Product Service Delivery Framework, as part of managing one or more software backlogs.
• Development of Solutions Architectures which utilize emerging and/or disruptive technologies (e.g. Artificial Intelligence (AI))
• Developing web-applications, APIs, and other software consistent with concurrently developed emergent architectures.
• Developing software documentation
• Developing and implementing an improved, modern data architecture while maintaining interim compatibility with a to-be-retired database

10. Options for SSO integration

3.3.2 Proposal Section 7.0 – Implementation Approach
This section shall provide a clear, comprehensive description of how the Proposer will plan, execute, manage, and support the implementation of the City's Cloud-Based Unified Communications system. Proposers must clearly define the roles and responsibilities of both the Proposer and the City throughout the project lifecycle.
1. Implementation Plan Overview:
Proposers shall describe their overall implementation methodology, including:
a) Alignment with Project Scope: Confirm all key tasks, milestones, and deliverables align with the requirements and phases outlined in Section 2 – Project Scope, of this RFP.
b) Implementation Activities: Describe the implementation activities for the UC system, including voice, messaging, collaboration, and mobile access; associated hardware such as desk phones, cordless phones, and conference devices; and any hosted call center or contact center functionality included in the proposed solution.
c) Deliverables and Outcomes: Explain the primary deliverables for each implementation phase and how they support successful, timely deployment of the UC system.
2. Project Management Services:
Proposers shall describe their project management approach, including:
a) Project Manager Role: Define the responsibilities of the Proposer's Project Manager, including coordination, scheduling, communications, issues resolution, risk management, and status reporting.
b) Project Tracking and Collaboration Tools: Identify whether a collaboration platform or tool will be used to track tasks, milestones, documentation, and approvals.
c) Quality Assurance and Controls: Describe the quality assurance, review, and escalation procedures used to ensure the project is delivered on time, within scope, and within budget.

3.4 Security and Privacy
Securing NASA data and commands, and the integrity of the overall system/service is mandatory. The NEXUS System will implement comprehensive cybersecurity, information assurance, cryptographic, and cloud security controls across the entire service architecture, encompassing space, ground, network, and cloud elements, including all prime contractor, subcontractor, service provider, hosted payload provider, ground segment operator, and third-party entity components. The provider will be required to complete the following activities and provide NASA with the artifacts called out in this section to enable NASA to determine the adequacy and acceptability of the provider's security control implementation.
Security Architecture Disclosure (CASQ): All candidates must complete the Commercial Architecture Security Questionnaire (CASQ) to ensure alignment with federal cybersecurity requirements.
Control Baseline and Evidence: The provider will provide evidence of cybersecurity control implementation satisfying High-impact requirements for availability and integrity consistent with FIPS 199 categorization. The provider shall implement and maintain information security controls in accordance with NIST SP 800-53 (current revision) consistent with FISMA and applicable NASA policy.
The system and associated services shall meet:
• High impact level controls for Integrity and Availability
• Moderate impact level controls for Confidentiality
The provider shall implement all required management, operational, and technical safeguards consistent with the applicable control baseline. The provider may demonstrate compliance using recognized industry cybersecurity frameworks or certifications (e.g., CMMC, ISO/IEC 27001, FedRAMP, or equivalent).

NGA manages a suite of tools, referenced throughout this document, across all security domains for building, deploying, and operating software:
► NGA's enterprise-managed version control instance is GitLab.
• NGA recommends using the Semantic Versioning method to version application source code.
► NGA's enterprise-managed CI/CD pipeline is available using both Jenkins and GitLab CI. For new projects, GitLab CI is recommended.
• Within NGA's enterprise-managed CI/CD pipeline, there are a number of other tools. Fortify, OWASP ZAProxy, and SonarQube are used for security and quality checks, and Threadfix is used for aggregating and managing these scans.
• Selenium and Puppeteer are commonly used tools within this pipeline for testing browser features.
• Terraform and/or CloudFormation are used though this pipeline to build infrastructure, and Ansible is frequently used for application deployments and configuration management.
► NGA's enterprise-managed work tracking and collaboration tools are Jira and Confluence. Additionally, RocketChat is commonly used for collaboration across teams and during incidents, although it tends to be best for ephemeral conversations rather than as a long-term source of truth for documentation.
► NGA's enterprise web-analytics software is Matomo.
As these specific tools change over time, such as if NGA chooses to use another tool with a similar or better capability, this appendix will be updated. The current deployed version of each tool, as well as more details on these and other available tools, can be found on the DevX Portal.
